zh.lifeSummary: 當Condesa Ava Pérez de Traba於大約1075年出生在葡萄牙桑塔雷姆時，她的父親Pedro Fróilaz de Traba, de Trastámara21歲，母親Urraca de Froilaz D'Aragón15歲。她和Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。

nameMeanings.name: Pérez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Francisco, Pedro, Miguel, Jorge, Ramon, Rafael, Mario.
Spanish (Pérez): patronymic from the personal name Pedro, an equivalent of Peter . This surname is also found in France. Compare De Perez .

variants.name: Parez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish Pérez (see Perez ).
In some cases possibly also French (Nord), Walloon, and Flemish: variant of Paré (see Pare ) or Paret .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ernst, Fritz, Heinz, Helmut, Horst, Kaspar, Klaus, Siegfried, Wolfgang.

English, Scottish, German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), Czech (Moravian), Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Péter): from the personal name Peter (Greek Petros, from petra ‘rock’). The personal name was popular throughout Christian Europe in the Middle Ages, having been bestowed by Christ as a byname on the apostle Simon bar Jonah, the brother of Andrew. The name was chosen by Christ for its symbolic significance (John 1:42, Matt. 16:18); Saint Peter is regarded as the founding head of the Christian Church in view of Christ's saying, ‘Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build my Church’. In Christian Germany in the early Middle Ages this was the most frequent personal name of non-ancient Germanic origin until the 14th century. In North America, this surname has also absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Czech Petr , Polish Piotr and Pietr, Albanian Pjetri (from the personal name Pjetër, definite form Pjetri), and also their derivatives (see examples at Peterson ). It has also been adopted as a surname by Ashkenazic Jews.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Traba
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from a farm name in Trøndelag, derived from trane ‘crane’. — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 423, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: Ava
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of uncertain origin, probably Germanic, from a short form of various female compound names containing the element av (of uncertain meaning). St Ava or Avia was a 9th-century abbess of Dinart in Hainault and a member of the Frankish royal family. However, evidence for the existence of the name between the early Middle Ages and the mid-20th century is lacking, and it may well be a modern invention. Its popularity since the 1950s is largely due to the film actress Ava Gardner ( 1922–90 ).

historicEvents.description: Afonso inherits the County of Portugal, a province of Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Condesa Ava Pérez de Traba was born about 1075, in Santarém, Portugal, her father, Pedro Fróilaz de Traba, de Trastámara, was 21 and her mother, Urraca de Froilaz D'Aragón, was 15. She had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Condesa Ava Pérez de Traba est née environ 1075, à Santarém, Portugal, son père, Pedro Fróilaz de Traba, de Trastámara, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Urraca de Froilaz D'Aragón, avait 15 ans. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera.

ko.lifeSummary: Condesa Ava Pérez de Traba(이)가 대략 1075년 포르투갈 산타e에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Pedro Fróilaz de Traba, de Trastámara(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Urraca de Froilaz D'Aragón(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 Conde Caballero Garcia Ordoñez de Aza Y de Nájera(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.
